title: Phaseolus glabellus
taxonomy: Phaseolus glabellus is classified as a species of wild bean in the family Fabaceae. It is native to North America, specifically in the eastern and central regions. This species is closely related to other wild beans and is part of the Phaseolus genus, which includes common beans and other related species. The classification of Phaseolus glabellus is further divided into the binomial nomenclature, with its genus and species names providing a unique identification. The classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this wild bean species.

intro: Phaseolus glabellus, also known as the smooth ground bean, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, due to its high content of fiber, protein, and antioxidants. The bioactive constituents of Phaseolus glabellus include isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Phaseolus glabellus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of menopause.
